<!-- Generated by AgentReady (LLM-first mode) | Dynamic section: do not edit manually. Static section in agent-context.json: safe to edit. -->

# Agent Working Memory Schema — agent-ready

This file defines the YAML structure an AI agent should maintain as working memory
while operating on this repository. Each session should instantiate a fresh copy of
this schema and populate it as work progresses.

---

## How to Use This Schema

1. At session start, copy the template below into your scratchpad/working memory.
2. Update fields as you complete work — especially `session_state.phase` and `files_modified`.
3. Before ending a session, populate `open_questions` with anything requiring human review.
4. Never commit this populated memory to the repository — it is ephemeral session state.

---

## Memory Template (YAML)

```yaml
# ============================================================
# AGENT WORKING MEMORY — agent-ready
# Instantiate fresh each session. Do NOT commit populated state.
# ============================================================

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. SESSION STATE
# Tracks where the agent is in the current task.
# ------------------------------------------------------------
session_state:
  task: ""                        # Human-readable description of the current goal
                                  # e.g. "Add async support to analyser.py"

  status: idle                    # One of: idle | in_progress | blocked | review_needed | done

  pipeline_phase: null            # Which AgentReady pipeline phase is being worked on, if any.
                                  # One of: null | phase_1_collect | phase_2_analyse |
                                  #         phase_3_generate | phase_4_score | phase_5_evaluate
                                  # See domain_concepts for phase definitions.

  active_component: null          # The key_component currently being modified.
                                  # One of: null | CLI | Analyser | Generator | Evaluator |
                                  #         Reviewer | ToolTemplates | InstallWorkflow |
                                  #         ReusableTransformer | DriftDetector

  active_file: null               # Absolute or repo-relative path of the file currently open/editing.
                                  # e.g. "src/agent_ready/analyser.py"

  provider_context:               # Which LLM provider/model is in use for this session's work.
    preset: null                  # e.g. "anthropic" | "openai" | "google" | "groq" | null
    model_override: null          # Set if --model flag was used; overrides all three phases.
                                  # WARNING: --model overrides analysis/generation/evaluation phases
                                  #          simultaneously — see potential_pitfalls.

  dry_run: false                  # True if agent-ready was invoked with --dry-run.
                                  # Dry-run means no files are written to disk.

  eval_only: false                # True if invoked with --eval-only (Phase 5 only, no generation).

  started_at: null                # ISO-8601 timestamp when this session began. e.g. "2025-01-15T10:30:00Z"
  last_updated_at: null           # ISO-8601 timestamp of the most recent memory update.


# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. DECISIONS MADE
# Record every non-trivial decision with full rationale.
# This prevents re-litigating the same question and gives
# human reviewers a clear audit trail.
# ------------------------------------------------------------
decisions_made:
  - id: null                      # Short unique slug. e.g. "use-litellm-retry-wrapper"
    timestamp: null               # ISO-8601 when the decision was made.
    component: null               # Which key_component this decision affects.
    file_affected: null           # Repo-relative path, if applicable.
    decision: ""                  # One sentence: what was decided.
    rationale: ""                 # WHY — cite code evidence, constraints, or domain_concepts.
                                  # e.g. "analyser.py already wraps calls in exponential backoff
                                  #       for 429/529 errors (see potential_pitfall #4); adding
                                  #       a second retry layer would double the wait time."
    alternatives_considered: []   # List of other options that were rejected.
    confidence: null              # high | medium | low
    requires_human_confirmation: false  # Set true if this decision has risk or ambiguity.


# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. FILES MODIFIED
# Track every file touched in this session with enough context
# to write a meaningful commit message and review the diff.
# ------------------------------------------------------------
files_modified:
  - path: null                    # Repo-relative path. e.g. "src/agent_ready/generator.py"
    component: null               # Which key_component this file belongs to.
                                  # Cross-reference with module_layout in agent-context.json.
    operation: null               # One of: created | modified | deleted | renamed
    change_summary: ""            # One-line human-readable summary of what changed.
    change_detail: ""             # Longer explanation if needed — mention WHY, not just WHAT.
    restricted: false             # Set TRUE if this path appears in restricted_write_paths.
                                  # restricted_write_paths: LICENSE, VERSION,
                                  # .github/workflows/ci.yml, .github/workflows/release.yml,
                                  # .github/workflows/codeql.yml
                                  # Modifying these without explicit approval is FORBIDDEN.
    is_template_file: false       # Set TRUE if path is under src/agent_ready/templates/.
                                  # Template files use {{PLACEHOLDER}} syntax — NOT Jinja2.
                                  # Ruff linting is intentionally skipped for these files.
    affects_version_sync: false   # Set TRUE if change touches src/agent_ready/__init__.py
                                  # or the VERSION file. Both must stay in sync — the release
                                  # workflow manages this; do not edit VERSION manually.
    test_coverage_impact: null    # increased | decreased | unchanged | unknown
                                  # CI enforces --cov-fail-under=50; track if changes risk this.


# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 4. TESTS RUN
# Record every test invocation so the agent knows current
# pass/fail state and whether coverage threshold is met.
# ------------------------------------------------------------
tests_run:
  - command: ""                   # Exact command executed.
                                  # Standard command: pytest -q --cov=src/agent_ready
                                  #   --cov-report=term-missing --cov-fail-under=50
    timestamp: null               # ISO-8601 when the test run completed.
    outcome: null                 # One of: passed | failed | error | skipped
    total_tests: null             # Integer count of tests collected.
    passed: null                  # Integer count of passing tests.
    failed: null                  # Integer count of failing tests.
    errors: null                  # Integer count of test errors (not assertion failures).
    coverage_pct: null            # Float. e.g. 73.4
    coverage_threshold_met: null  # Boolean. CI threshold is 50% (--cov-fail-under=50).
    failed_test_ids: []           # List of pytest node IDs that failed.
                                  # e.g. ["tests/test_analyser.py::test_collect_skips_agent_files"]
    notes: ""                     # Anything notable — flaky tests, mocking issues, etc.
                                  # NOTE: Tests mocking LLM calls must account for the retry
                                  #       wrapper (exponential backoff for 429/529 errors).


# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 5. OPEN QUESTIONS
# Items the agent cannot resolve alone — needs human input,
# external context, or explicit approval before proceeding.
# ------------------------------------------------------------
open_questions:
  - id: null                      # Short slug. e.g. "confirm-version-bump-strategy"
    timestamp: null               # ISO-8601 when this question was raised.
    question: ""                  # The specific question needing an answer.
    context: ""                   # Why this question arose — what the agent was doing.
    blocking: false               # True if work cannot continue until this is answered.
    category: null                # One of: approval_needed | clarification | design_decision |
                                  #         external_dependency | security_review
    suggested_resolution: ""      # Agent's best guess — human can confirm or override.


# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 6. DOMAIN STATE
# Python/LLM-specific state worth tracking across steps.
# Grounded in the actual domain_concepts and architecture
# of the agent-ready pipeline.
# ------------------------------------------------------------
domain_state:

  # --- Python / Build Environment ---
  python_env:
    install_mode: null            # One of: null | "core" | "ai_extra"
                                  # "core" = pip install -e .  (stdlib only, no litellm)
                                  # "ai_extra" = pip install -e '.[dev]'  (includes litellm)
                                  # CRITICAL: litellm is an OPTIONAL extra — importing
                                  # agent_ready LLM features without [ai] will ImportError.
    litellm_available: null       # Boolean — whether litellm can be imported in this env.
    active_provider_keys_set: []  # List of env vars confirmed present in this shell.
                                  # Subset of: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY,
                                  # GOOGLE_API_KEY, GROQ_API_KEY, MISTRAL_API_KEY,
                                  # TOGETHER_API_KEY
    ruff_version: null            # e.g. "0.4.2" — ruff is the linter/formatter for this project.

  # --- AgentReady Pipeline Execution State ---
  pipeline_run:
    target_repo: null             # Path to the repository being transformed, if running the tool.
    phases_completed: []          # Ordered list of completed phases from:
                                  # [phase_1_collect, phase_2_analyse, phase_3_generate,
                                  #  phase_4_score, phase_5_evaluate]
    analysis_json_produced: false # True once Phase 2 (Analyse) has emitted structured JSON.
    scaffolding_files_written: [] # List of files written by Phase 3 (Generate).
                                  # Expected set: [AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, system_prompt.md,
                                  #   agent-context.json, memory/schema.md]
    readiness_score_before: null  # Integer 0–100 from Phase 4 before generation.
    readiness_score_after: null   # Integer 0–100 from Phase 4 after generation.
    evaluation_questions_asked: null   # Integer count from Phase 5 (should be 15).
    evaluation_categories_covered: [] # Subset of: [commands, safety, domain, architecture, pitfalls]

  # --- Scaffolding / Context Drift State ---
  context_drift:
    drift_detected: false         # True if the drift detector workflow flagged staleness.
    last_context_refresh: null    # ISO-8601 date of most recent successful scaffolding generation.
    stale_files: []               # Which scaffolding files are suspected stale.
                                  # Any of: AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, system_prompt.md,
                                  #         agent-context.json, memory/schema.md

  # --- Circular Analysis Guard ---
  skip_agent_files_respected: null  # Boolean — confirms the analyser is skipping
                                    # AgentReady-generated files (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md,
                                    # agent-context.json, etc.) via SKIP_AGENT_FILES set.
                                    # Feeding them back would create circular analysis.

  # --- PR Reviewer State (if running in --review mode) ---
  pr_review:
    pr_number: null               # Integer PR number from PR_NUMBER env var.
    review_posted: false          # True once the review comment was posted via GitHub API.
    gh_token_available: null      # Boolean — GH_TOKEN present in environment.
    review_grounded_in_context: null  # Boolean — confirms agent-context.json was loaded
